title: Claude Code rule format compatibility
purpose: Users should be able to drop Claude Code rule files (with globs: frontmatter) into .norn/rules/ without modification. This brief adds format detection and auto-mapping to the rules parser so both Norn's native format and Claude Code's simpler format are supported.
task: Modify rules/parser.rs to detect the rule format by examining frontmatter keys. When triggers: is present, use the existing Norn parser path. When globs: or paths: is present, auto-map to Norn types: each glob becomes a PathGlob trigger, delivery defaults to SystemContextAppend, timing defaults to After, description maps to Rule.name. When both triggers: and globs:/paths: are present, return a parse error.

requirements.title: Norn format detection
requirements.spec: WHEN the frontmatter YAML contains a triggers: key, the parser SHALL use the existing Norn format parsing path.
acceptance: A file with triggers: key is parsed via the existing path
All existing rules/parser.rs tests continue to pass unchanged
The detection checks for key presence, not key value

requirements.title: Claude Code format detection
requirements.spec: WHEN the frontmatter YAML contains a globs: or paths: key (and no triggers: key), the parser SHALL use the Claude Code compatibility path.
acceptance: A file with globs: key and no triggers: key is detected as Claude Code format
A file with paths: key and no triggers: key is detected as Claude Code format
paths: is treated as an alias for globs:

requirements.title: Ambiguous format rejection
requirements.spec: IF the frontmatter contains both triggers: and globs: (or paths:), parse_rule_file() SHALL return RulesError::ParseFailed with a message indicating ambiguous format.
acceptance: A file with both triggers: and globs: returns RulesError::ParseFailed
The error message contains 'ambiguous' or similar indication
A file with triggers: and paths: also returns an error

requirements.title: Single glob string mapping
requirements.spec: WHEN globs: is a single string, it SHALL be mapped to one TriggerCondition::PathGlob with that string as the pattern.
acceptance: globs: '**/*.rs' produces one PathGlob trigger with pattern '**/*.rs'
The resulting rule has exactly one trigger in its triggers vec

requirements.title: Glob array mapping
requirements.spec: WHEN globs: is an array of strings, each string SHALL be mapped to a separate TriggerCondition::PathGlob.
acceptance: Each trigger has the correct pattern from the array

requirements.title: Description to Rule.name mapping
requirements.spec: WHEN the Claude Code format frontmatter contains a description: field, it SHALL be mapped to Rule.name.
acceptance: description: 'Rust coding standards' sets Rule.name to 'Rust coding standards'
When description: is absent, Rule.name defaults to empty string

requirements.title: Implicit delivery and timing defaults
requirements.spec: Claude Code format rules SHALL default to DeliveryMode::SystemContextAppend and TriggerTiming::After.
acceptance: A Claude Code format rule has delivery == DeliveryMode::SystemContextAppend
A Claude Code format rule has timing == TriggerTiming::After
These defaults are applied regardless of other frontmatter fields

boundaries: SHALL NOT modify the Rule type or TriggerCondition type — only the parser
SHALL NOT modify the RuleEngine — it already accepts any well-formed Rule
SHALL NOT add new trigger types for Claude Code compatibility
verification: cargo test -p norn -- rules::parser passes all tests (existing + new)
All existing Norn-format rule tests pass unchanged (backward compatible)
No file exceeds 500 lines of code (excluding tests, comments, whitespace)
